violations.text: The establishment does not employ an individual with the Rhode Island Manager Certified In Food Safety license. Establishment has individuals with active food safety certificate. Establishment to register. Application provided during of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 01/10/2026

Black Beans and Pinto Beans were observed held at 121-122 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Additional water was added to steam table and foods were reheated to a minimum of 165 degrees Fahrenheit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025

Some staff were observed not wearing effective hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025
Plastic plate for cheese grating machine was observed cracked in 2 places. Equipment to be in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 01/10/2026

The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. Lids covered during time of inspection. Accumulation of debris was observed in enclosure. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/12/2025

-The coving baseboard in the dry storage room was observed in poor repair -The coving and wall by back door was observed in poor repair -Crack in ceiling observed above cashier area (PIC states when it rains it actively leaks). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026

Some areas of floors in the kitchen were observed in poor condition. Sealant observed in poor repair affecting cleanability. PIC informed. (This violation does not exceed original correct by date) New Violation. Correct By: 01/13/2026

Cooked chicken on both lines was observed held between 121-130 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were assessed and reheated during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/13/2025
Caulking at handsink near cookline was observed in poor repair resulting in black residue accumulation. Equipment to be in good repair and proper adjustment. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2025
Toilet in women's room was not operational during time of inspection. Repairs in progress. Per PIC, facility is awaiting on maintanence. New Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2025
Some areas of floors in the kitchen were observed in poor condition. Sealant observed in poor repair affecting cleanability. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 01/13/2026
Hole observed at exterior of backdoor leading to hallway. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. -Establishment shall register a ServSafe certified employee as the Certified Food Safety Manager with RIDOH Center for Food Protection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
